examples.title: Knowledge-Based Agent
examples.code:
FUNCTION KB-AGENT(percept):
 STATIC: KB, a knowledge base
 t, a counter (time step)
 
 TELL(KB, MAKE-PERCEPT-SENTENCE(percept, t))
 action ← ASK(KB, MAKE-ACTION-QUERY(t))
 TELL(KB, MAKE-ACTION-SENTENCE(action, t))
 t ← t + 1
 RETURN action

# Basic inference using Modus Ponens
FUNCTION INFER(KB, premise, implication):
 IF premise ∈ KB AND (premise → implication) ∈ KB THEN
 TELL(KB, implication)
 RETURN implication
examples.explanation: Shows TELL/ASK architecture for KB agents. TELL adds percepts and actions to KB, ASK queries for best action based on current knowledge.

examples.title: Forward Chaining Algorithm
examples.code:
FUNCTION FORWARD-CHAIN(KB, goal):
 agenda ← [facts from KB]
 inferred ← empty set
 
 WHILE agenda is not empty:
 p ← POP(agenda)
 IF p NOT IN inferred THEN
 ADD p TO inferred
 # For each rule (p1 ∧ ... ∧ pn) → q where p matches premise
 FOR EACH rule WHERE p IS A premise:
 IF ALL OTHER premises in inferred THEN
 q ← CONCLUDE(rule)
 ADD q TO agenda
 TELL(KB, q)
 
 RETURN goal IN inferred
examples.explanation: Starts from known facts, applies rules to derive new facts until goal is proven. Efficient for Horn clause KBs.

examples.title: Backward Chaining Algorithm
examples.code:
FUNCTION BACKWARD-CHAIN(KB, goal):
 IF goal IN KB THEN RETURN true
 IF goal IS A FACT THEN RETURN false
 
 # For each rule that concludes goal
 FOR EACH rule (p1 ∧ ... ∧ pn) → goal:
 FOR EACH premise pi IN rule:
 IF NOT BACKWARD-CHAIN(KB, pi) THEN
 RETURN false
 RETURN true
 
 RETURN false

# With memoization
FUNCTION ASK-BC(KB, goal):
 IF goal IN known_true THEN RETURN true
 IF goal IN known_false THEN RETURN false
 result ← BACKWARD-CHAIN(KB, goal)
 ADD result TO cache
 RETURN result
examples.explanation: Starts from goal, recursively proves sub-goals. Useful for query answering, avoids irrelevant work.

examples.title: Resolution Refutation Proof
examples.code:
# Convert KB to CNF
FUNCTION RESOLUTION-PROVE(KB, goal):
 # Negate the goal
 negated_goal ← NOT(goal)
 clauses ← CNF(KB ∪ {negated_goal})
 
 # Resolution loop
 WHILE new_clauses_generated:
 FOR EACH pair (Ci, Cj) IN clauses:
 resolvents ← RESOLVE(Ci, Cj)
 IF empty_clause IN resolvents THEN
 RETURN true # Contradiction found
 ADD resolvents TO clauses
 
 RETURN false # No contradiction - goal not proved

# Unification helper
FUNCTION UNIFY(x, y, theta):
 IF theta THEN return failure
 IF x = y THEN return theta
 IF VARIABLE(x) THEN return UNIFY-VAR(x, y, theta)
 IF VARIABLE(y) THEN return UNIFY-VAR(y, x, theta)
 IF COMPOUND(x) THEN return UNIFY(x.args, y.args, theta)
 RETURN failure
examples.explanation: Complete inference method for FOL. Proves goal by showing KB plus negated goal is inconsistent (empty clause = contradiction).
